title: Resilient Coders Fall 2026 Fellowship: Apply for a Free Full Stack Engineering Bootcamp and Launch Your Software Engineering Career
summary: Resilient Coders is now accepting applications for its Fall 2026 Full Stack Engineering Fellowship , a fully funded, intensive software engineering training programme designed to prepare talented individuals from underrepresented backgrounds for high-growth careers in technology. The fellowship is completely free of charge and offers participants an opportunity to gain practical software engineering skills, receive mentorship from experienced professionals, collaborate with peers, and build a strong foundation for employment in the tech industry. For over a decade, Resilient Coders has helped participants launch successful careers at leading companies such as Audible, CarGurus, Accenture, Liberty Mutual , and many other technology-focused employers. Interested applicants must first submit an Interest Form to gain access to the official Fall 2026 application. The extended application deadline is July 10, 2026 . About the Resilient Coders Fellowship Resilient Coders is committed to creating opportunities for individuals from underrepresented communities to access rewarding careers in software engineering. The Full Stack Engineering Fellowship provides participants with intensive technical training while helping them develop professional skills, confidence, and industry connections. The programme combines: Live instructor-led training Hands-on coding projects Team collaboration Professional mentorship Career preparation Networking opportunities Portfolio development Graduates leave the programme equipped with practical software engineering skills and the experience necessary to pursue employment in the technology sector. Fellowship Benefits Participants selected for the Fall 2026 cohort will receive numerous benefits, including: Free Full Stack Engineering training Live instruction from experienced software engineers Remote learning from anywhere within the eligible U.S. regions Mentorship from industry professionals Community support throughout the programme Collaborative coding experience Hands-on software development projects Interview preparation Career readiness support Networking opportunities with technology professionals Opportunity to join a growing alumni network Increased access to careers at top technology companies Over the past ten years, Resilient Coders alumni have secured employment at organizations including: Audible CarGurus Accenture Liberty Mutual And many other leading employers. Programme Schedule The Fall 2026 Fellowship follows a structured full-time schedule. Programme Dates August 3, 2026 – December 18, 2026 Duration 18 weeks Full-time Fully remote Class Schedule Monday – Friday 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM (EST) Community Mentor Sessions Tuesdays and Thursdays 3:30 PM – 6:00 PM (EST) Applicants should note that additional programme expectations exist beyond the scheduled class hours. Who Can Apply? Priority consideration is given to applicants who: Are between 18 and 35 years old. Come from low-income households based on HUD guidelines. Are passionate about software engineering. Are ready to commit to an intensive learning experience. Are eager to build a long-term career in technology. Individuals over the age of 35 are still encouraged to apply. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria: Be authorized to work in the United States. Live in: Greater Boston Greater Philadelphia Maine New York Hold at least a High School Diploma or GED. Not possess a Master s degree or higher. Meet the programme s low-income eligibility requirements. Commit to the full programme schedule. Application Process Applicants should carefully complete each stage of the selection process. Step 1: Watch the Introduction Video Prospective applicants begin by watching the one-hour HTML introduction video. Step 2: Submit an Interest Form Submitting the Interest Form allows applicants to: Receive recruitment updates. Access the official application. Receive important reminders and deadlines. Applicants are encouraged to monitor their email after submitting the Interest Form. Step 3: Complete the Official Application Applicants should prepare: An updated resume. A LinkedIn profile. Applications must be completed before the deadline. Step 4: Complete the Responsive Web Design Course Applicants are encouraged to complete the Responsive Web Design course available on freeCodeCamp. This course provides foundational knowledge that prepares participants for success during the fellowship. Step 5: Participate in a Hackathon Eligible applicants will receive an invitation to participate in a required coding hackathon. During the hackathon, participants will: Work in teams. Build a website. Collaborate with alumni facilitators. Demonstrate coding skills. Learn about the student experience. Participation in the hackathon is a mandatory part of the application process. Step 6: Interview Semi-finalists will be invited to participate in an interview with a member of the Resilient Coders programme team. Step 7: Paperwork and Onboarding Finalists will submit documentation verifying: Age Address Financial eligibility Employment authorization Following successful onboarding, participants will prepare to begin classes. Why Choose Resilient Coders? The fellowship offers much more than technical instruction. Participants gain: Real-world coding experience Professional mentorship Industry exposure Career coaching Collaborative learning Access to a strong alumni network Skills aligned with employer needs By the end of the programme, graduates are well positioned to pursue careers as software engineers and developers across the technology industry. Important Dates Applicants should note the following timeline: Application Opens: April 1, 2026 Extended Application Deadline: July 10, 2026 Programme Begins: August 3, 2026 Programme Ends: December 18, 2026 How to Apply Interested applicants must first submit the Interest Form to receive access to the official application for the Fall 2026 Fellowship. After submitting the Interest Form, applicants should regularly check their email for instructions from the Resilient Coders admissions team. For additional information and to begin your application, visit the official Resilient Coders website. Application Process Watch our 1 hour long introduction the HTML video Submit an Interest Form ‍Sign up to get access to the application and to receive recruitment alerts about the application process. Complete the Application before the deadline You will need a resume and LinkedIn to complete the application. ‍Start working on the Responsive Web Design Course on freeCodeCamp This course is meant to provide you with a strong base to hit the ground running on the first day of classes. Code With Us If you meet our eligibility criteria, you will be invited to participate in one of our hackathons. Attending a hackathon is a required step of the application process. You’ll get a chance to work with a group facilitated by one of our program alumni to create a website. This is an opportunity to test your coding skills while learning more about the Resilient Coders student experience. Schedule an Interview Semi-finalists will be will be invited to schedule an interview call with a program team member. Paperwork Onboarding Finalists will be asked to submit documentation verifying your age, address, financial statement, and employment eligibility. Get ready to start classes! Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Resilient Coders Fall 2026 Fellowship: Apply for a Free Full Stack Engineering Bootcamp and Launch Your Software Engineering Career appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: GIZ South Africa is Hiring! Apply for Three Exciting Career Opportunities under the CPD4E Project Before July 2026
summary: The Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) has announced three exciting career opportunities under its Career Path Development for Employment (CPD4E) Project , providing experienced professionals with the chance to contribute to South Africa s employment creation and Just Energy Transition agenda. Implemented in partnership with the Department of Higher Education and Training (DHET) , the CPD4E project focuses on improving employment opportunities for young South Africans by strengthening skills development, supporting enterprise growth, and preparing the workforce for the country s transition to a decarbonized economy. The project is currently recruiting qualified candidates for three technical positions that will contribute to employment promotion, skills development, entrepreneurship, and labour market readiness across South Africa. Interested applicants should note that each vacancy has its own application deadline ranging from 15 July 2026 to 17 July 2026 . About the Career Path Development for Employment (CPD4E) Project The Career Path Development for Employment (CPD4E) Project is designed to improve the employment prospects of young people in South Africa by aligning education, skills development, and labour market needs within a rapidly changing economy. Implemented by GIZ in collaboration with the Department of Higher Education and Training (DHET) , the programme supports South Africa s transition toward a greener, more sustainable economy while creating inclusive employment opportunities. The project focuses on: Improving youth employability. Supporting employment-oriented private sector development. Enhancing Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET). Promoting entrepreneurship and Small, Medium and Micro Enterprises (SMMEs). Supporting the Just Energy Transition through skills development. Strengthening labour market readiness. Available Positions 1. Senior Technical Advisor: SMME Development and Support (Output 2) This position will focus on promoting employment through enterprise development by supporting small businesses and encouraging private sector growth. Key responsibilities include: Supporting employment-oriented private sector development. Promoting entrepreneurship and SMME growth. Developing initiatives that create employment opportunities. Working closely with stakeholders to strengthen business ecosystems. Supporting sustainable economic development initiatives. 2. Senior Technical Advisor: Employment Support through Skills Development (Outputs 3 4) This role supports skills development initiatives aimed at improving employment outcomes across South Africa. The successful candidate will: Support programme development within the TVET sector. Assist with implementation of new skills programmes. Contribute to the Team Europe Initiative focusing on the battery value chain. Strengthen employment pathways through skills development. Collaborate with education and training stakeholders. 3. Technical Advisor: JET-Skills (Seconded to the Presidential Climate Commission) This position supports South Africa s Just Energy Transition (JET) agenda by strengthening workforce readiness for the country s evolving economy. Responsibilities include: Coordinating JET Skills projects. Supporting implementation of the Presidential Climate Commission s Just Transition agenda. Managing projects related to labour market readiness. Developing employment pathways. Supporting skills development initiatives linked to climate transition priorities. Working closely with government and strategic partners. Why Work with GIZ? GIZ is one of the world s leading international development organizations, working with governments and partners to promote sustainable development worldwide. Joining the CPD4E project offers professionals the opportunity to: Work on nationally significant employment initiatives. Contribute to South Africa s Just Energy Transition. Support youth employment and economic development. Collaborate with government institutions and international partners. Gain experience on high-impact development programmes. Build expertise in labour market and skills development initiatives. Join a globally respected development organization. Application Requirements Applicants should ensure they submit: A complete application. An updated CV. A motivation letter. Applications submitted without a motivation letter will not be considered . Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Application Deadlines Applicants should carefully observe the individual closing dates for each position. Senior Technical Advisor: SMME Development and Support Application Deadline: 17 July 2026 Senior Technical Advisor: Employment Support through Skills Development Application Deadline: 16 July 2026 Technical Advisor: JET-Skills Application Deadline: 15 July 2026 Early applications are encouraged. Why You Should Apply These positions offer an outstanding opportunity to contribute to South Africa s future workforce by supporting employment, entrepreneurship, skills development, and climate transition initiatives. Professionals passionate about youth employment, sustainable development, labour market transformation, and public-private collaboration are encouraged to submit their applications before the respective deadlines. Learn More More global Jobs opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post GIZ South Africa is Hiring! Apply for Three Exciting Career Opportunities under the CPD4E Project Before July 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: International Fund for Public Interest Media Opens 2026 Grant Call: Up to USD 200,000 for Independent Media in Armenia, Moldova, and Ukraine
summary: The International Fund for Public Interest Media (IFPIM) has officially launched a new regional funding opportunity for independent media organizations across Armenia, Moldova, and Ukraine . Through its latest Open Call, titled Strengthening What Works: Investing in the Future of Public Interest Media in Armenia, Moldova, and Ukraine, the Fund seeks to strengthen financially sustainable journalism and support media organizations committed to serving the public interest. Announced on 7 July 2026 , this initiative is designed to help independent media consolidate successful business models, expand sustainable revenue streams, improve organizational resilience, and continue delivering quality journalism despite an increasingly challenging financial environment. Organizations selected through the programme are expected to receive two-year grants , with an average funding amount of approximately USD 200,000 , depending on the size, capacity, and demonstrated needs of each organization. The deadline to complete the Eligibility Verification Form is July 12, 2026 . About the International Fund for Public Interest Media Grant Programme Independent journalism remains one of the pillars of democratic societies. However, many media organizations continue to face severe financial pressures, particularly following the funding crisis experienced in 2025. To address these challenges, IFPIM has launched this regional grant programme to ensure that successful independent media organizations can continue operating while improving their long-term financial sustainability. Rather than simply providing emergency funding, the programme focuses on investing in organizations that have already demonstrated resilience and now seek to strengthen operations through innovative and sustainable approaches. The grant programme aims to help organizations: Improve financial resilience. Consolidate proven business models. Diversify revenue sources. Adapt to changing audience behavior. Invest in innovation. Strengthen organizational capacity. Continue producing high-quality public interest journalism. Grant Value Successful applicants will receive: Approximately USD 200,000 in grant funding on average. Funding for a two-year implementation period . Grant amounts tailored to each organization s: Size Operational capacity Financial needs Long-term sustainability Applicants should note that IFPIM funding may represent no more than 30% of an organization s average annual turnover . Organizations are therefore encouraged to prepare realistic funding requests aligned with their financial capacity and long-term strategy. Objectives of the Programme The International Fund seeks to strengthen independent journalism by supporting organizations that can: Deliver trusted public interest journalism. Improve organizational sustainability. Strengthen financial management. Diversify funding sources. Develop innovative business models. Build resilience against future funding shocks. Adapt to rapidly changing media markets. Continue serving audiences vulnerable to misinformation and disinformation. Who Should Apply? The programme is designed for independent media organizations operating in: Armenia Moldova Ukraine Eligible organizations should already demonstrate: A strong record of quality journalism. Financial management capacity. Commitment to editorial independence. Long-term sustainability planning. Readiness to implement a comprehensive two-year operational strategy. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must first complete the secure Eligibility Verification Form . Organizations meeting the eligibility requirements will receive an invitation to submit a full funding proposal through the International Fund portal. One important financial eligibility condition states that: Annual International Fund support must not exceed 30% of the organization s average annual turnover . Selection Process The grant selection process consists of several stages. Step 1: Eligibility Verification Applicants submit responses through the Eligibility Verification Form. Step 2: Full Application Eligible organizations receive access to the International Fund application portal where they submit: Full proposal Organizational documents Supporting information Step 3: Initial Review The International Fund reviews applications to verify: Eligibility Completeness Required documentation Programme alignment Step 4: Independent Expert Evaluation An external panel of independent media experts evaluates proposals using predefined assessment criteria. Step 5: Board Approval Recommended organizations are submitted to the International Fund Board for final approval before grants are awarded. Selection Criteria Applications will be assessed across three major areas. 1. Public Interest Journalism and Audience Impact Reviewers will consider: The organization s role in informing the public. Audience reach. Service to underserved communities. Quality journalism. Contribution to combating disinformation. Organizations working in areas especially vulnerable to foreign influence and misinformation may receive particular attention. 2. Quality of the Two-Year Operational Plan Applicants should present a clear and realistic strategy showing how grant funding will: Strengthen operations. Improve organizational capacity. Stabilize finances. Diversify revenue. Increase resilience. Adapt to changing market conditions. 3. Organizational Strength and Financial Sustainability Reviewers will evaluate: Leadership capacity. Financial health. Long-term sustainability. Ability to remain relevant. Limited access to commercial or donor funding. Overall resilience under difficult market conditions. Journalism Trust Initiative (JTI) Support Beyond financial support, successful applicants may also receive funding to pursue Journalism Trust Initiative (JTI) certification. The Journalism Trust Initiative is an internationally recognized standard developed by Reporters Without Borders (RSF) that helps news organizations demonstrate: Editorial transparency. Ethical journalism practices. Professional standards. Public accountability. Through a partnership between IFPIM and RSF, successful grantees choosing to pursue certification will receive: Funding for consulting services. Certification fee coverage. Access to qualified consultants. Guidance throughout the certification process. Important Dates Applicants should carefully follow the programme timeline. July 12, 2026 – Deadline to complete the Eligibility Verification Form. July 26, 2026 – Deadline to submit organizational profile documents through the International Fund portal. August 2, 2026 – Deadline to submit the complete funding proposal. November 18, 2026 – Successful applicants notified of grant awards. How to Apply The application process begins with the Eligibility Verification Form. Eligible organizations will subsequently receive access to the International Fund portal to submit their complete proposals. Applicants should prepare all organizational documents well in advance to ensure timely submission. Contact Information For technical assistance related to the grant portal: Email: grants@ifpim.org For programme-related questions: Contact: Kateryna Chystoforova, IFPIM Program Associate Why This Opportunity Matters Independent journalism remains essential for democratic governance, accountability, transparency, and informed public debate. Through this regional funding programme, the International Fund for Public Interest Media is investing not only in media organizations but also in stronger, more resilient information ecosystems across Armenia, Moldova, and Ukraine. By supporting sustainable business models, encouraging innovation, and strengthening editorial independence, the programme aims to ensure that trusted public interest journalism continues to thrive despite financial and political challenges. Organizations that meet the eligibility requirements are encouraged to submit their applications before the relevant deadlines. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Grants opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post International Fund for Public Interest Media Opens 2026 Grant Call: Up to USD 200,000 for Independent Media in Armenia, Moldova, and Ukraine appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: France Launches 2026 Small Grants Programme: Receive Up to €4,600 for Civil Society Projects in the Caribbean
summary: The French Regional Department of Cooperation and Cultural Action (SCAC) has officially launched the 2026 Small Grants Programme , offering civil society organizations an excellent opportunity to secure funding for innovative development initiatives across the Eastern Caribbean and neighboring countries. The programme is designed to strengthen local organizations by financing projects that address important social, environmental, cultural, and economic challenges while promoting sustainable development throughout the region. Since its creation in 2021 , the Small Grants Programme has successfully supported 40 impactful projects across the 11 countries covered by SCAC. The 2026 edition continues this commitment by providing financial assistance to organizations working to create positive change in their communities. Organizations interested in applying must submit their complete applications no later than August 16, 2026 . About the France 2026 Small Grants Programme The Small Grants Programme is an initiative of the Regional Department of Cooperation and Cultural Action (SCAC), which covers: Eastern Caribbean States (OECS) Barbados Jamaica Trinidad and Tobago Suriname Guyana The programme seeks to empower civil society organizations by supporting innovative local solutions that contribute to sustainable development and stronger communities. Selected projects may receive funding covering up to 85% of the total project cost , with a maximum grant amount of €4,600 . Projects selected under the programme must be implemented between: September 2026 June 21, 2027 Funding Benefits Successful applicants will enjoy several important benefits, including: Funding of up to 85% of total project costs Maximum financial support of €4,600 Opportunity to implement community-focused development projects Increased visibility for participating organizations Support from the French Regional Department of Cooperation and Cultural Action Opportunity to contribute to regional sustainable development priorities Strengthened partnerships across the Caribbean region Priority Themes Projects submitted under the 2026 call must focus on one or more of the following priority areas: 1. Ocean Projects promoting: Marine conservation Ocean health Biodiversity protection Coastal ecosystem preservation Sustainable marine resource management 2. Sports Projects that: Encourage sports participation Promote youth engagement through sports Improve community well-being Foster inclusion through sporting activities 3. Cultural and Creative Industries Initiatives supporting: Creative entrepreneurship Cultural heritage Arts and culture Creative economy development 4. Francophonie Projects that: Promote the French language Strengthen Francophone cooperation Encourage cultural exchanges Support French-speaking communities 5. Youth Support Projects designed to: Empower young people Build leadership skills Improve youth participation Increase educational and professional opportunities 6. Sustainable Agriculture Projects focusing on: Climate-smart agriculture Sustainable farming Food security Agricultural innovation Environmental sustainability 7. Women s Empowerment Initiatives promoting: Gender equality Women s economic participation Women s leadership Social inclusion Capacity development Cross-Cutting Themes Applications incorporating one or both of the following themes will receive special consideration during evaluation: Gender Marker Projects that demonstrate meaningful gender equality outcomes and inclusive participation. Regional Integration Projects encouraging cooperation among Caribbean countries, particularly those involving partnerships with the French overseas Caribbean territories. Project Timeline Successful projects must follow the implementation schedule below: Application Deadline: August 16, 2026 Project Start: September 2026 Project Completion: June 21, 2027 Information Session Applicants who wish to better understand the application process are encouraged to participate in the free online information session. Date: Monday, July 13, 2026 Time: 3:00 PM Duration: Approximately one hour The session will explain: Eligibility requirements Application procedures Required documents Evaluation process Frequently asked questions Required Application Documents Applicants must carefully read the programme s Terms of Reference before preparing their proposals. The following documents are required: Terms of Reference Completed Application Form Completed Budget Template Incomplete applications may not be considered. How to Apply Applicants should prepare all required documents and submit their completed application by email. Submission Email: teamscac@gmail.com Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues. Contact for Enquiries For additional information regarding the programme, applicants may contact: André ZOZOR Technical Cooperation Officer Email: andre.zozor@diplomatie.gouv.fr Application Deadline The deadline for submitting complete applications is: August 16, 2026 Late applications are unlikely to be considered. Why You Should Apply The France 2026 Small Grants Programme provides an outstanding opportunity for civil society organizations to transform innovative ideas into impactful community projects. With funding covering up to 85% of project costs, organizations can implement initiatives that promote sustainable development, strengthen local communities, empower women and youth, protect the environment, encourage cultural development, and foster regional cooperation throughout the Caribbean. Organizations working in any of the programme s priority sectors are strongly encouraged to submit competitive proposals before the application deadline. For complete programme guidelines, application documents, and additional information, visit the official programme page. Documents to download and complete to apply : Application form Budget The closing date for submitting complete applications by email to teamscac@gmail.com is August 16, 2026. Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Grants opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post France Launches 2026 Small Grants Programme: Receive Up to €4,600 for Civil Society Projects in the Caribbean appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Spark Change-Makers Project: Comprehensive Guide to the Youth-Led & Youth-Focused CSO/Social Enterprise Application (Cohort 1)
summary: The Spark Change-Makers Project, part of the EU–Ghana Youth Empowerment Programme, is a major initiative designed to strengthen youth-led and youth-focused Civil Society Organisations (CSOs) and Social Enterprises across Ghana. Implemented by SOS Children’s Villages Ghana in partnership with Songtaba and Social Innovation Africa, this programme equips young leaders with the skills, knowledge, and resources needed to improve livelihoods and drive sustainable development. This article provides a detailed breakdown of the application form, eligibility requirements, thematic focus areas, and expectations for organisations seeking to join Cohort 1 of the capacity-strengthening phase. Overview of the Spark Change-Makers Project According to the document: “The Spark Change-makers Project is a European Union co-funded initiative designed to empower young people (18-35) by equipping them with skills, knowledge, and resources to improve their livelihoods and strengthen their participation in sustainable development.” The project operates across six regions: Greater Accra Ashanti Volta Northern Western Upper East Eligible organisations may progress to a grant-support phase after completing the capacity-strengthening stage. Eligibility Summary To qualify, organisations must meet the following criteria: Must be youth-led, youth-focused, or a social enterprise. Must operate in at least one of the six implementing regions. Must demonstrate strong community engagement. Must show commitment to inclusion, sustainability, and environmentally friendly solutions. Section-by-Section Breakdown of the Application Form SECTION A: Introduction Consent Applicants provide basic organisational details including: Name of organisation Region of operation Community name Organisation email Primary contact person Phone number Type of organisation (CSO or Social Enterprise) Youth leadership/focus category SECTION B: Organisational Profile Legal Status Options Registered Not registered Informally organised and operating for at least 12 months Mission/Goal of the Organisation Applicants must clearly state their mission and long-term goals. Organisational Documents Systems Applicants indicate whether they possess: Strategic Plan Constitution or By-laws Financial Policy Prior donor funding Monitoring Evaluation system Thematic Areas (Must Select at Least One) Organisations must directly address at least one of the following themes: Digital Technology Gender Youth Empowerment Culture Creative Industries Agriculture Environmental Protection Peace Security Applicants must provide one concrete example of work done in the selected theme(s). Youth Leadership Requirements The document states: “The CEO / Executive Director must be aged 18-35, OR the majority of the Management must be aged 18-35, OR the organisation s primary mission and at least 80% of its activities must directly benefit young people aged 18-35.” Applicants must also provide statistics on: Board members Staff Youth staff Youth in leadership SECTION C: Experience Impact Applicants must describe: Key activities completed in the last year Number of beneficiaries served Evidence of impact (reports, documentation, digital content) Social media handles SECTION D: Thematic Focus Community Impact Applicants must explain: The specific social problem addressed Primary beneficiaries and their community Significant activities completed in the last 12 months Why the community needs the organisation’s work Sustainability measures SECTION G: Capacity Needs Applicants must identify: Three major capacity gaps How the Spark Change-Makers Project will address these gaps Sustainability plans after the project ends SECTION H: Inclusion Equity Applicants indicate whether they serve: Young women Young people with disabilities Urban or rural communities Environmental sustainability Safeguarding policies They must also describe how their work promotes gender equality and addresses discrimination. SECTION J: Regional Validation Applicants confirm: Primary region of operation Whether they operate in multiple regions SECTION K: Declaration Applicants must confirm the accuracy of their information and agree to project terms. The document states: “Submitting this application does not guarantee selection or grant funding.” They must also confirm compliance with EU grant regulations and SOS CV Ghana’s data protection policy. Deadline Application Deadline: 31st July 2026 For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here Discover more training opportunities here The post Spark Change-Makers Project: Comprehensive Guide to the Youth-Led Youth-Focused CSO/Social Enterprise Application (Cohort 1) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNICEF StartUp Lab Reducing Single-Use Plastics through WASH & Social and Behaviour Change Innovation Challenge 2026: Win $5,000, Pilot Your Innovation in Ghana, and Help Protect Children’s Health
summary: Applications are now open for the UNICEF StartUp Lab Reducing Single-Use Plastics through Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) and Social Behaviour Change (SBC) Innovation Challenge 2026 . This innovation challenge is inviting startups, innovators, entrepreneurs, social enterprises, and changemakers to develop practical, scalable, and sustainable solutions that reduce the use of single-use plastics within Ghana s WASH sector while promoting healthier communities and protecting children from the harmful effects of plastic pollution. Selected innovators will receive funding, mentorship, technical support, and the opportunity to pilot their solutions within UNICEF WASH and community programmes. Application Deadline: July 23, 2026 About the UNICEF StartUp Lab WASH Innovation Challenge Plastic pollution has become one of the biggest environmental and public health challenges affecting Ghana today. Single-use plastics are widely used across the country, particularly within water, sanitation, and hygiene services. Products such as: Plastic water sachets Plastic packaging Disposable hygiene products Other single-use plastic materials continue to accumulate in communities due to inadequate collection and disposal systems. Improper disposal of these plastics blocks drainage systems, pollutes rivers and streams, contaminates soil, and contributes significantly to greenhouse gas emissions. The challenge goes beyond environmental concerns. Poor waste management creates stagnant water that becomes breeding grounds for mosquitoes, increasing the spread of malaria and other water-borne diseases. Burning plastic waste also contributes to poor air quality, increasing respiratory illnesses among vulnerable populations, particularly children. As plastics gradually degrade into microplastics, they contaminate water sources and food systems, posing long-term risks to public health and food security. Recognizing these growing challenges, UNICEF StartUp Lab is seeking innovative solutions that promote a circular economy while transforming how communities use, collect, reuse, replace, reduce, and recycle plastics. Why This Challenge Matters Current plastic waste management systems are not sufficient to address the growing environmental crisis. The UNICEF StartUp Lab seeks to encourage innovative approaches that move communities away from the traditional use-and-dispose model toward sustainable circular systems. The challenge combines innovation with Social and Behaviour Change (SBC) approaches to encourage lasting behavioural shifts that reduce dependence on disposable plastics. Ultimately, the initiative aims to improve sanitation systems, protect the environment, strengthen climate resilience, and improve health outcomes for children. Objectives of the Challenge The challenge seeks innovative solutions that can: Reduce dependence on single-use plastics. Promote responsible plastic collection and disposal. Encourage reuse, reduction, replacement, and recycling using the 3Rs. Improve sanitation and hygiene services. Strengthen community participation in sustainable waste management. Protect children from diseases associated with plastic pollution. Support climate-resilient WASH systems. Develop scalable and affordable innovations that can be implemented across Ghana. Challenge Themes Applicants may address one or more of the following innovation areas. 1. Alternative Solutions Develop affordable, accessible, and scalable alternatives to single-use plastics used within water, sanitation, and hygiene services. Solutions should promote: Reduction Replacement Reuse Recycling while remaining practical for local communities. 2. Behavioural Integration Design Social and Behaviour Change (SBC) strategies that encourage households, institutions, and communities to reduce their reliance on disposable plastics. Innovations may include: Behaviour change campaigns Digital engagement platforms Community education programmes Incentive systems School-based interventions 3. Circular Systems Develop closed-loop systems that improve the collection, reuse, reduction, replacement, recycling, or responsible disposal of plastics throughout the WASH value chain. Potential innovations may include: Plastic collection systems Community recycling programmes Deposit-return schemes Incentive-based recycling models Waste recovery technologies Circular business models 4. Climate Resilience Create innovative WASH infrastructure and services capable of reducing the environmental impacts of plastic pollution while protecting communities from climate-related risks. Solutions should improve both environmental sustainability and child health. What UNICEF Is Looking For UNICEF is seeking practical innovations capable of significantly reducing or eliminating single-use plastics from household and community environments. Successful applicants should demonstrate their ability to: Develop Sustainable Alternatives Innovations should introduce practical alternatives to disposable plastics that are affordable, locally available, and environmentally sustainable. Drive Behavioural Change Applicants should demonstrate how Social and Behaviour Change methodologies will encourage communities and service providers to adopt sustainable practices. Improve Community Health Solutions should reduce children s exposure to pollution caused by plastic waste while supporting healthier sanitation systems. Deliver Scalable Models Innovations should be: Affordable Scalable Adaptable Easy to integrate into existing government or community WASH programmes Benefits for Selected Innovators Successful applicants will receive: US$5,000 cash prize Opportunity to pilot their innovation within UNICEF WASH and community programmes Mentorship from experts Data support to strengthen and validate their innovation Opportunity to build scalable and cost-effective solutions Alignment with Ghana s National Plastic Management Policy Access to public-sector WASH implementation opportunities Strategic Importance This challenge supports several national and global development priorities, including: Ghana s National Plastic Management Policy Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 6 – Clean Water and Sanitation Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 13 – Climate Action The programme also strengthens collaboration between startups, innovators, government institutions, and development partners to create sustainable environmental solutions that directly improve children s wellbeing. Expected Outcomes Selected innovations will be piloted within existing UNICEF WASH programmes and community initiatives. The pilot projects will generate evidence demonstrating how innovative approaches can: Reduce plastic waste. Improve waste collection systems. Encourage behavioural change. Strengthen sanitation infrastructure. Improve environmental sustainability. Protect children s health. Create scalable models for wider implementation across Ghana. The long-term vision is to build a portfolio of proven innovations capable of transforming plastic waste management while creating healthier communities and more resilient WASH systems. Why You Should Apply If your startup or innovation focuses on sustainability, waste management, climate action, circular economy, sanitation, recycling, behaviour change, environmental technology, or WASH, this challenge offers an excellent opportunity to test your innovation in a real-world environment. Beyond the financial award, successful innovators will gain valuable pilot experience, expert mentorship, technical support, and the opportunity to contribute to national environmental priorities while improving the lives of children across Ghana. Application Deadline July 23, 2026 How to Apply Interested applicants can apply for the UNICEF StartUp Lab HERE . Click HERE for more information on the UNICEF StartUp Lab. Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here . The post UNICEF StartUp Lab Reducing Single-Use Plastics through WASH Social and Behaviour Change Innovation Challenge 2026: Win $5,000, Pilot Your Innovation in Ghana, and Help Protect Children s Health appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully‑Funded PhD Program in Biomedical Sciences at the Max Delbrück Center (MDC), Berlin
summary: The Max Delbrück Center (MDC) in Berlin stands as one of Europe’s leading research institutions in biomedical sciences, offering an exceptional, fully‑funded PhD program designed for ambitious young scientists who want to explore the foundations of human health and disease. With cutting‑edge facilities, world‑class mentors, and a deeply collaborative research culture, the MDC PhD Program provides an outstanding environment for scientific growth and career development. Program Overview The MDC PhD Program is a structured, research‑intensive doctoral training pathway that supports students in conducting innovative biomedical research across multiple disciplines. The program focuses on three major scientific themes: Genes, Cells and Cell‑based Medicine Molecular Processes and Therapies Integrative Biomedicine These themes reflect MDC’s mission to advance tomorrow’s medicine through discovery, translational science, and interdisciplinary collaboration. What the Program Offers Doctoral researchers at MDC benefit from a comprehensive training ecosystem designed to support both scientific excellence and personal development. Key features include: International Research Environment — Work alongside scientists from more than 70 countries. Individualized Supervision Mentoring — Each student receives guidance from a dedicated thesis committee. Advanced Scientific Training — Access to workshops, lectures, journal clubs, and specialized courses. State‑of‑the‑Art Infrastructure — Cutting‑edge laboratories, core facilities, and technology platforms. Participation in Specialized Research Programs — Including MDC‑BIMSB, ECRC, and HI‑TAC. Career Development Support — Structured programs for leadership, communication, and scientific growth. Travel Grants — Funding for external conferences, workshops, and courses. Active Alumni Network — Long‑term professional connections worldwide. Optional Enrollment in HEIBRiDS Data Science School — For students interested in computational biology and AI. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must hold (or expect to obtain within four months after the interview week) a Master’s degree or equivalent in one of the following fields: Biology Biotechnology Medicine Molecular Medicine Chemistry Computer Science Data Science Engineering Mathematics Physics Or a closely related discipline Applicants should demonstrate strong motivation for biomedical research and readiness to engage in interdisciplinary scientific work. Application Process The MDC conducts central recruitment once every Fall , and applications are accepted exclusively through the online portal. Application Steps Register on the Application Portal Submit required documents (CV, transcripts, motivation letter, references). Select preferred research topics and group leaders. Participate in online interviews (October). Successful candidates begin their PhD in January , with flexibility up to four months after interviews . Deadline: The next call opens June 29, 2026 , and remains open through August 2026. Application deadline: August 24, 2026 Funding Employment Doctoral researchers receive: A fully‑funded 3‑year employment contract (often extended to 4 years). Salary according to 65% TVöD 13 , approximately 1800 EUR net per month . Full health and social insurance coverage. Access to university enrollment with semester fees of 300–320 EUR . Available Projects A complete list of participating group leaders and project descriptions becomes accessible upon registration in the application portal. Projects span molecular biology, genomics, computational biology, cardiovascular research, regenerative medicine, and more. Timeline (Approximate) Call Open: June – August Online Interviews: October Start Date: January (or up to 4 months after interviews) Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more fellowship opportunities here The post Fully‑Funded PhD Program in Biomedical Sciences at the Max Delbrück Center (MDC), Berlin appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AWLN 8th Intergenerational Retreat 2026 in Gabon: Fully Sponsored Leadership Opportunity for Young African Women Leaders and Diaspora Changemakers
summary: The African Women Leaders Network (AWLN), jointly supported by the African Union (AU) and the United Nations (UN), has officially opened applications for its prestigious 8th Intergenerational Retreat (IGR 8) . This flagship leadership programme will bring together 40 outstanding young African women leaders from across the African continent and the Diaspora for a fully sponsored retreat in Libreville, Gabon , in December 2026. Designed as a platform for mentorship, leadership development, networking, and collaboration, the AWLN Intergenerational Retreat connects emerging women leaders with influential policymakers, Heads of State, business executives, and experienced leaders who are shaping Africa s future. The retreat aligns with the African Union s Agenda 2063 and the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), strengthening women s leadership and accelerating gender equality, peace, governance, and sustainable development across Africa. Application Deadline: 03 August 2026 About the African Women Leaders Network (AWLN) The African Women Leaders Network (AWLN) is a joint initiative of the African Union (AU) and the United Nations (UN) that seeks to increase women s leadership and participation in peacebuilding, governance, economic development, and sustainable development across Africa. Since its establishment, AWLN has built a powerful network of women leaders spanning government, diplomacy, civil society, academia, entrepreneurship, youth movements, and international organizations. The network promotes intergenerational dialogue, women s empowerment, mentorship, and collaborative leadership to address Africa s most pressing social, economic, and political challenges. About the AWLN 8th Intergenerational Retreat (IGR 8) The Intergenerational Retreat is AWLN s signature leadership programme that brings together established and emerging women leaders to exchange ideas, strengthen leadership skills, and build meaningful partnerships. Rather than functioning as a traditional conference, the retreat serves as a launchpad for long-term collaboration, mentorship, and action-oriented leadership. The 2026 edition will take place in Libreville, Gabon , bringing together 40 selected young women leaders from across Africa and the African Diaspora. Participants will engage in interactive sessions, mentoring activities, leadership dialogues, collaborative workshops, and networking opportunities aimed at strengthening women s leadership across generations. Why the AWLN Intergenerational Retreat Matters Women continue to play a transformative role in advancing peace, governance, economic growth, climate action, entrepreneurship, and community development across Africa. However, many emerging women leaders face challenges such as limited access to mentorship, leadership opportunities, decision-making spaces, and regional networks. The AWLN Intergenerational Retreat addresses these challenges by creating a platform where experienced African women leaders can mentor and support the next generation of changemakers. The programme contributes directly to: Advancing women s leadership across Africa. Promoting gender equality and inclusion. Strengthening regional collaboration. Supporting young women in influencing policy and governance. Building sustainable leadership pipelines. Encouraging innovation and community impact. Objectives of the Retreat The AWLN 8th Intergenerational Retreat aims to: Strengthen women s leadership across Africa. Build the leadership capacity of emerging women leaders. Promote mentorship between senior and young women leaders. Foster intergenerational dialogue. Encourage collaboration across sectors and countries. Support implementation of Agenda 2063 and the Sustainable Development Goals. Create lasting partnerships that contribute to Africa s transformation. What Participants Will Gain Successful applicants will receive a fully sponsored opportunity to participate in one of Africa s leading women leadership programmes. Benefits include: 1. Fully Sponsored Participation Selected participants will receive: Full registration International or regional travel support Accommodation throughout the retreat This ensures participants can fully engage in the programme without financial barriers. 2. Mentorship from Influential Women Leaders Participants will interact directly with high-profile women leaders, including: Heads of State Vice Presidents Ministers CEOs Senior policymakers Development leaders Influential African women leaders These mentoring opportunities provide practical guidance, leadership insights, and career inspiration. 3. Leadership Development Participants will strengthen skills in areas such as: Strategic leadership Public speaking Policy engagement Advocacy Decision-making Collaboration Negotiation Community leadership 4. Pan-African Networking The retreat provides access to an extensive network of women leaders from: Governments Civil society International organizations Academia Private sector Youth movements African Diaspora communities Participants will establish professional relationships that extend well beyond the retreat. 5. AWLN Leadership Award Selected participants will also have the opportunity to receive the prestigious AWLN Leadership Award, recognizing outstanding commitment to leadership and community impact. 6. Continued Support Participants become part of AWLN s growing community and benefit from ongoing engagement through: Networking opportunities Leadership initiatives Knowledge sharing Mentorship Future regional collaborations Retreat Themes The programme focuses on strengthening leadership across AWLN s six strategic pillars: 1. Peace and Security Leadership initiatives that contribute to conflict prevention, peacebuilding, and security. 2. Governance and Political Participation Promoting women s participation in governance, leadership, policymaking, and democratic institutions. 3. Financial Inclusion, Economic Development, Natural Resources and Mining Supporting women s economic empowerment, entrepreneurship, financial inclusion, and sustainable resource management. 4. Agriculture, Rural Economy and Land Strengthening women s leadership in agriculture, food systems, rural development, and land governance. 5. Social Mobilisation Encouraging community engagement, advocacy, civic participation, and social transformation. 6. Youth and Emerging Leaders Empowering young women to become influential leaders across Africa. Who Should Apply? The programme seeks dynamic young African women leaders who have already demonstrated positive impact within their communities. Applicants should be passionate about creating lasting change and advancing women s leadership. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be aged between 18 and 45 years . Be an African woman residing on the continent or in the African Diaspora. Demonstrate measurable or recognized impact in their community. Be actively involved in one or more of AWLN s six thematic pillars. Show commitment to leadership, collaboration, and community development. What AWLN Expects from Participants The retreat is designed to create long-term impact rather than simply host an event. Selected participants are expected to: Participate actively in all sessions. Contribute ideas and experiences. Support intergenerational dialogue. Build meaningful collaborations. Promote AWLN s mission within their communities. Apply lessons learned after the retreat. Continue contributing to the AWLN network beyond the programme. AWLN emphasizes that participants are selected not only because of their achievements, but also because of their willingness to inspire others and contribute to Africa s collective development. Why You Should Apply The AWLN Intergenerational Retreat is one of the continent s premier leadership programmes for emerging African women leaders. Successful participants will have the opportunity to: Learn directly from influential African women leaders. Expand professional and regional networks. Strengthen leadership capacity. Gain international visibility. Collaborate with women leaders from across Africa and the Diaspora. Influence conversations on Africa s future. Contribute to advancing gender equality and sustainable development. For young women committed to creating meaningful change, the retreat provides a powerful platform to accelerate both personal and professional growth. Application Process Interested applicants should submit their applications online through the Official AWLN Application Platform . Applicants are encouraged to ensure that: All required information is completed accurately. Applications are submitted before the deadline. Supporting information clearly demonstrates leadership achievements and community impact. Only complete applications will be reviewed. Application Deadline Application Deadline: 03 August 2026 Late or incomplete applications may not be considered. How to Apply Applicants should: Visit the Official AWLN application Portal . Complete the online application form. Submit all required information before 03 August 2026 . Await communication from the AWLN Secretariat regarding the selection process. For additional information or inquiries, applicants may visit the AWLN official website HERE . Conclusion The AWLN 8th Intergenerational Retreat 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for young African women leaders to strengthen their leadership abilities, expand their regional and international networks, and collaborate with some of Africa s most influential women leaders. By bringing together emerging changemakers and experienced decision-makers, the retreat creates a powerful environment for mentorship, innovation, and collective action that supports Africa s long-term development goals. For women who are passionate about governance, peacebuilding, entrepreneurship, agriculture, social mobilisation, community development, or youth leadership, this fully sponsored programme provides an invaluable platform to grow, inspire others, and contribute meaningfully to the continent s future. Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare their applications early and submit them before the 03 August 2026 deadline. Discover more global African , Fellowship , Women , and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY. The post AWLN 8th Intergenerational Retreat 2026 in Gabon: Fully Sponsored Leadership Opportunity for Young African Women Leaders and Diaspora Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: University of Messina PhD Scholarships 2026–2027 in Italy | Fully Funded & Funded Doctoral Programs for International Students Across 15 Research Fields
summary: The University of Messina has officially opened applications for its 42nd Cycle PhD Programs (Academic Year 2026/2027) , offering an exceptional opportunity for graduates from around the world to pursue doctoral studies in one of Italy s historic public universities. The competition invites applications for 15 doctoral (PhD) programmes covering engineering, medicine, economics, psychology, political science, sustainability, humanities, chemistry, veterinary sciences, cognitive sciences, law, public administration, physics, biology, and many other disciplines. Numerous fully funded PhD scholarships , executive doctoral positions, industry-funded research scholarships, and non-scholarship places are available. Successful candidates will undertake a three-year doctoral programme beginning on 1 November 2026 , conducting advanced research under internationally recognized academic supervisors while contributing to innovative scientific and societal solutions. The University of Messina welcomes applications from Italian and international candidates and offers several funding opportunities supported by the University, the Italian Ministry of University and Research (MUR), private companies, national agencies, and regional funding programmes. About the University of Messina PhD Programs The University of Messina is one of Italy s leading public universities with a strong international reputation in research, innovation, medicine, engineering, humanities, social sciences, and environmental studies. For the 2026/2027 academic year , the university is opening its 42nd Cycle of PhD programmes , providing candidates with opportunities to: Conduct cutting-edge scientific research Work alongside internationally recognized professors Collaborate with research institutes and private industries Participate in international mobility programmes Receive generous doctoral scholarships Build careers in academia, research, government, NGOs, healthcare, and industry The programmes officially commence on 1 November 2026 and run for three academic years . Application Deadline Application Deadline: 24 July 2026 (11:59 PM Italy Time) Applicants must complete and submit their online application before the deadline through the University s Esse3 platform. Late applications will not be accepted. Duration The PhD programmes last: Three years Starting on 1 November 2026 Available PhD Programmes Applications are open for the following doctoral programmes. 1. Bioengineering Applied to Medical Sciences Research areas include: Biomedical engineering Medical technologies Medical imaging Artificial intelligence in healthcare Dentistry Biotechnology Biomedical innovation Funding includes: 4 University/MUR scholarships 1 position without scholarship 2. Applied Biology and Experimental Medicine Curricula include: Experimental Medicine Biological and Environmental Sciences Pharmaceutical Sciences Funding: 4 scholarships 1 non-funded position 3. Economics, Management and Statistics Curricula include: Economics Management Statistics Special opportunities include: Executive PhD positions AI for biomedical management Sustainable innovation Circular economy research Corporate sustainability Industry partners include: Biofuture Medicine DPT Consulting Funding includes: 4 scholarships Executive industry PhD opportunities 2 non-funded positions 4. Physics Research areas include: Quantum physics Materials Astrophysics Experimental physics Theoretical physics Funding: 4 scholarships 1 non-funded position 5. Engineering One of the largest doctoral programmes available. Research areas include: Mechanical Engineering Computer Engineering Civil Engineering Electrical Engineering Cybersecurity Artificial Intelligence Naval Engineering Renewable Energy Hydrogen Technology Materials Engineering Telecommunications Aerospace Engineering Environmental Engineering Industry-funded scholarships are available through: National Cybersecurity Agency Novamarine Vishay Semiconductor Zappettificio Muzzi HYDRAE Research Project Several scholarships include: International mobility Industry placements Company-based research Overseas research periods 6. Psychology Research focuses include: Clinical Psychology Educational Psychology Cognitive Psychology Health Sciences Rehabilitation Funding: University scholarships University of Catanzaro scholarships 7. Chemical Sciences Research areas include: Pharmaceutical Chemistry Food Chemistry Molecular Systems Functional Materials Nutraceuticals Funding: 4 scholarships 1 non-funded position Additional PhD Programmes The call also includes doctoral opportunities in: Cognitive Sciences Public Administration Sciences Legal Sciences Political Science Humanities Veterinary, Biotechnological and Agricultural Sciences Sustainability Sciences Translational Molecular Medicine and Surgery Each programme has its own admission criteria, curriculum, examination procedures, and scholarship allocations. What the Scholarships Cover Many successful applicants will receive fully funded PhD scholarships. Benefits include: Annual scholarship worth €16,243 Monthly scholarship payments Research funding International mobility support Opportunities for overseas research Access to advanced laboratories Academic supervision International collaborations Industry partnerships Professional research training Additional research budgets are available for approved international research stays. Scholarship recipients may also receive increased funding for authorized periods spent conducting research abroad. Research Opportunities Doctoral candidates will have opportunities to: Publish scientific papers Present at international conferences Conduct interdisciplinary research Collaborate with international universities Work alongside private industries Participate in EU-funded research Develop patents and innovations Gain teaching experience Build international academic networks Who Can Apply? Applications are welcomed from: Italian students European Union citizens International students from all countries There are no age restrictions and no nationality restrictions for standard admission to the PhD programmes. Applicants must satisfy the academic requirements specified for their chosen programme. APPLY HERE Eligibility Requirements Applicants should possess one of the following: Master s Degree Equivalent postgraduate qualification Equivalent foreign degree recognized for admission Students completing their Master s degree may also apply provided they obtain the qualification before 31 October 2026 . Some programmes require: Specific academic backgrounds English language proficiency Italian language proficiency Research proposal Professional qualifications depending on the programme Certain Executive PhD positions require employment with affiliated companies, while high-level apprenticeship positions have additional age requirements. Learn More Here Required Application Documents Applicants generally need to submit: Europass or academic CV Master s degree certificate or equivalent documentation Academic transcripts Research proposal (where required) Master s thesis Publications (if available) Professional experience documents Identity document Additional declarations and forms specified by the University International applicants with foreign qualifications must provide translated academic documents where required. Selection Process Admission is based on: Academic qualifications Research proposal (where applicable) Written examination (for selected programmes) Oral interview The evaluation process considers: Academic performance Research potential Publications Professional experience Motivation Language proficiency Many interviews may be conducted online or in mixed mode, depending on the programme. Scholarship Renewal Scholarships are renewable annually based on: Academic progress Research performance Positive evaluation by the PhD Board Completion of annual research activities International Student Information International applicants without Italy s SPID digital identity can register through the University s Esse3 platform using dedicated credentials. Students admitted from outside the European Union will also need to complete visa and pre-enrolment procedures through the Universitaly portal where applicable. Why You Should Apply The University of Messina PhD programmes offer much more than doctoral education. Participants will: Earn an internationally recognized Italian PhD Receive competitive financial support Conduct cutting-edge research Build international academic collaborations Gain teaching and research experience Publish scientific work Develop professional networks Access advanced laboratories Collaborate with industry partners Improve career prospects in academia, research institutions, government, NGOs, healthcare, engineering, and the private sector Visit HERE to learn more about the University of Messina PhD Scholarships 2026–2027 in Italy. Important Dates Call Published: 24 June 2026 Corrected Call Published: 7 July 2026 Applications Open: 25 June 2026 Application Deadline: 24 July 2026 (11:59 PM Italy Time) PhD Program Start Date: 1 November 2026 Conclusion The University of Messina s 42nd Cycle PhD Call represents one of Italy s most comprehensive doctoral recruitment opportunities for the 2026/2027 academic year. With programmes spanning science, engineering, medicine, humanities, social sciences, law, economics, sustainability, and technology, applicants can access world-class research environments, competitive scholarships, international collaborations, and valuable industry partnerships. Whether your ambition is to become an academic researcher, university lecturer, policy expert, scientist, engineer, healthcare innovator, or industry leader, these doctoral programmes provide the academic foundation and professional experience needed to advance your career on the global stage. Interested candidates are encouraged to review the specific requirements for their preferred PhD programme, prepare a strong application, and submit all required documents before the 24 July 2026 deadline. Discover more global Scholarship and Doctoral or PhD opportunities on OFY. title: Google Apprenticeships 2026–2027: Paid Work-and-Study Opportunities at Google Across Europe | Launch Your Career in Software Engineering, AI, Marketing, UX, Customer Engineering & More
summary: Google continues to invest in developing the next generation of technology professionals through its globally recognized Apprenticeship Program. The Google Apprenticeships 2026–2027 provide aspiring professionals with a unique opportunity to earn a salary while gaining practical work experience at one of the world s leading technology companies. Unlike traditional internships, Google s apprenticeship programs combine structured academic learning with on-the-job professional training. Participants work alongside experienced Googlers, receive external training through accredited education partners, and build practical skills that prepare them for long-term careers in technology, business, engineering, marketing, design, and customer solutions. Whether you are looking to transition into technology, gain industry-recognized experience, or begin a professional career while continuing your studies, Google Apprenticeships offer one of the most valuable work-and-study opportunities available. Applications are offered across multiple countries and disciplines throughout the year, with each apprenticeship following its own recruitment timeline. About Google Apprenticeships Google Apprenticeships are structured work-and-study programmes designed to help individuals develop technical and professional skills while earning an income. Rather than requiring participants to choose between education and employment, apprentices combine both experiences simultaneously. Throughout the programme, apprentices: Work as part of Google teams Receive practical, hands-on experience Complete formal external training Develop technical and professional competencies Build industry experience Receive coaching and career support Earn a salary throughout the programme Google emphasizes that apprentices bring diverse backgrounds and experiences, helping create products that better serve users worldwide. The company also promotes an inclusive workplace that values diversity and equal opportunity. Why Consider a Google Apprenticeship? Google Apprenticeships offer significantly more than workplace experience. Participants benefit from: Paid employment while learning Professional mentoring Exposure to Google s global work culture Hands-on project experience External accredited education Career coaching Networking opportunities Technical and business skill development Increased employability after graduation The programmes are ideal for individuals who prefer practical learning combined with formal education. What Apprentices Receive Successful applicants can expect numerous professional and personal development opportunities. Benefits include: 1. Paid Employment All Google apprenticeships are paid positions. Participants receive: Competitive salary Benefits Professional development support Employee learning resources Google confirms that every apprenticeship is a paid opportunity. 2. Real Google Work Experience Apprentices become active members of Google teams rather than simply observing workplace activities. Depending on the programme, participants may contribute to: Software development Customer engineering Digital marketing UX and media design Sales operations Data centre operations AI and machine learning projects Cloud technologies Communication campaigns 3. Structured External Training Each apprenticeship combines work experience with formal education delivered by accredited training providers, universities, or vocational institutions. Training may include: Classroom learning Technical bootcamps University coursework Vocational qualifications Industry certifications 4. Professional Skill Development Throughout the programme apprentices strengthen: Communication Leadership Teamwork Problem solving Project management Presentation skills Technical expertise Customer engagement 5. Career Coaching Google provides career coaching throughout the apprenticeship. Support includes: Career planning Resume development Interview preparation Professional networking Guidance for future employment Learn More Here Countries Offering Google Apprenticeships Google currently offers apprenticeship opportunities across several European countries. Available locations include: France Available programmes include: Sales and Marketing Apprenticeship Duration: 12 months Participants: Study at a university or engineering school Complete apprenticeship training through a Centre de Formation d Apprentis (CFA) Work part-time at Google Applications typically open during spring for autumn entry. Software Engineering Apprenticeship Duration: 12–36 months Focus areas include: Computer Science Software Engineering Technical development Participants combine university studies with practical software engineering experience. Customer Engineering Apprenticeship Duration: 24 months Study areas include: Computer Science Infrastructure Application Modernization Security AI and Machine Learning Data Science Networking Ireland Software Engineering Apprenticeship Duration: 24 months Programme includes: Six months of external training On-the-job experience at Google Partnership with FIT Google notes that hiring for the 2025 cohort has closed, with recruitment for the 2026 cohort beginning in February. Netherlands Data Center Technician Apprenticeship Programme duration varies between: 20–34 weeks Participants remain enrolled in school while completing practical workplace learning at Google s data centres. Switzerland Application Development Apprenticeship (EFZ) Duration: Four years The programme begins with one year of external training before apprentices join Google teams. Interactive Media Design Apprenticeship (EFZ) Duration: Four years Participants divide their time between: School (approximately 40%) Workplace learning (approximately 60%) United Kingdom Google has previously offered: Digital Business Apprenticeship Duration: 15 months Delivered in partnership with Makers. Although recruitment for the 2026 cohort has closed, similar opportunities may reopen in future cycles. Software Engineering Apprenticeship Duration: 24 months Includes: Fourteen-week technical bootcamp Workplace learning Level 4 Software Developer qualification Eligibility Requirements Eligibility varies depending on the apprenticeship and country. Generally, applicants should: Meet the minimum qualifications listed for the programme Be eligible to work in the country where the apprenticeship is located Meet educational requirements Submit all required application documents Google notes that apprenticeships are generally available to individuals aged 18 and above, except in Switzerland where local requirements differ. Can International Applicants Apply? Yes. International applicants may apply provided they: Have legal authorization to work in the country Meet programme requirements Secure any necessary visas where applicable Applicants are responsible for obtaining work authorization if required. Can Graduates Apply? Yes. Google welcomes applicants who already possess university degrees, provided the degree is in a different subject from the apprenticeship they wish to pursue. This makes apprenticeships an excellent pathway for career changers. Application Documents Applicants generally need to submit: Updated CV or resume Cover letter explaining motivation Additional documents required for the specific apprenticeship Applications that are incomplete will not be considered. Selection Process The recruitment process typically includes: Application review Recruiter screening Eligibility assessment Interviews or assessment centre Final selection Each apprenticeship may have slightly different procedures. Will Apprentices Receive Permanent Jobs? Google explains that apprenticeships are designed to prepare participants for entry-level careers. After completing the programme, apprentices may: Apply for full-time roles at Google Continue their education Pursue careers elsewhere in the technology industry Employment is not guaranteed but participants receive career coaching throughout the programme. Relocation Support Where eligible, Google may provide relocation assistance. Details are discussed during recruitment and offer stages. Support for Applicants with Disabilities Google provides accommodations for candidates with disabilities throughout the recruitment process. Accommodation requests are handled by Google s dedicated accessibility teams. Why Google Apprenticeships Stand Out Google Apprenticeships combine several advantages rarely found together: Paid employment Professional training World-class work experience Academic study Career coaching Networking opportunities Exposure to cutting-edge technologies Pathways into technology careers For individuals looking to begin or transition into careers in software engineering, AI, cloud computing, marketing, customer engineering, UX, or digital business, these programmes provide an exceptional opportunity to build practical experience while continuing formal education. Application Timeline Application dates vary depending on: Country Programme Training partner Many programmes recruit seasonally, with applications opening in spring or summer for autumn starts, while some programmes have specific annual recruitment windows. Applicants are encouraged to regularly monitor the Google Apprenticeships portal for new openings. Learn More Here How to Apply Interested applicants should: Visit Google s Apprenticeships Portal . Browse available programmes by country and profession. Review the eligibility requirements for their chosen apprenticeship. Prepare an updated CV/resume and tailored cover letter. Submit all required application materials before the relevant deadline. Because application windows differ across programmes, candidates should check frequently for newly opened roles. Conclusion Google Apprenticeships 2026–2027 represent one of the most attractive work-and-study opportunities available for aspiring professionals seeking practical experience at a global technology company. By combining paid employment, structured learning, mentorship, and career development, these programmes equip participants with the technical and professional skills needed to succeed in today s competitive job market. Whether your interests lie in software engineering, AI, customer engineering, marketing, UX design, digital business, or data centre operations, a Google Apprenticeship can serve as a powerful stepping stone toward a rewarding career in technology and innovation. Prospective applicants are encouraged to explore available programmes, prepare strong application materials, and monitor recruitment timelines for their preferred country and discipline. Discover more Global Internship opportunities on Opportunities for Youth (OFY) HERE .
